title: Release readiness production health input v1
description: Shape consumed by --prod-health. A snapshot of a production (or in-process) HTTP health probe, captured at evaluation time. The engine reads the boolean `healthy` (and falls back to `ok` for backward compatibility) to decide whether the prod_health validation passes; the remaining fields are surfaced in the report. Additional fields beyond those listed here are ignored. Reference probes: release-readiness-sample-app/cmd/prod-health-probe/main.go (Go), release-readiness-node-js-sample-app/scripts/prod-health-probe.ts (TypeScript).

url.description: The URL that was probed (full path including the health endpoint, e.g. https://example.com/healthz).

healthy.description: Required. Overall result. True when the probe got HTTP 200 AND the body parsed to {status: 'ok'}; false otherwise. The engine uses this as the prod_health validation's pass/fail signal.

ok.description: Backward-compatibility alias for `healthy`. Older probes used this field name; the engine accepts either.

http_status.description: HTTP status code returned by the probe. Null when the probe could not connect (e.g. connection refused).

latency_ms.description: Round-trip time in milliseconds. Null when the probe could not connect.

checked_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the probe ran (e.g. '2026-05-02T01:23:45.000Z').

source.description: Where the probe ran. 'external' = a real deployment (PROD_HEALTH_URL set); 'in_process' = a local in-process server spun up by the probe (sample/demo scope).

body.description: The response body. Parsed as JSON when possible; falls back to the raw string. Null on connection error.

error.description: Error message when the probe could not complete (e.g. 'connection refused'). Omitted on success.
